reasoning:
The baseline value on 2025-06-08 will be 3,361,938 (the June 4 value, published June 5). 
1. June 15 (evaluating June 11): Over the past 5 weeks, reserves have steadily climbed as the Treasury General Account (TGA) shrinks before mid-June tax day. This short-term momentum makes a continued increase likely. 
2. July 8 (evaluating July 2): Mid-June corporate tax receipts will swell the TGA, aggressively draining reserves (similar to the massive $480B plunge in late April). This strongly suggests a drop below the June baseline. 
3. Sept 6 & Dec 5: Ongoing Quantitative Tightening (QT) structurally removes liquidity as securities mature. Over 3 to 6 months, this secular drag makes it increasingly difficult to surpass the early June peak. However, as the forecast horizon extends, uncertainty regarding potential early cessation of QT or shifts in Fed policy increases, driving probabilities closer to 0.5 as dictated by the prompt limits.
